title: Multi-input operator foundations
summary: Defines rank-r tuples of ambient functions, rank-r operators, unary adapters, slot replacement, and slotwise unary mapping.

mathematicalContent.heading: Rank-r inputs and operators
mathematicalContent.body: A multi-input is an ordered r-tuple of ambient functions indexed by Fin r. A rank-r operator consumes such a tuple and returns one ambient function; no linearity or differential-order condition is imposed.

mathematicalContent.heading: Unary adapters
mathematicalContent.body: An ordinary unary operator can be viewed as a one-input multi-operator by reading slot zero. Conversely a one-input multi-operator becomes unary by filling its unique slot with f.

mathematicalContent.heading: Input transformations
mathematicalContent.body: replace changes one chosen slot, while mapUnary applies the same unary transformation independently to every slot.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines a rank-r ambient input as a function from the finite index set Fin r to ambient functions, equivalently an ordered r-tuple.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev MultiInput (_CalConf : Calculus Conf) (r : ℕ) : Type _

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines a rank-r ambient operator as an arbitrary map from r ambient inputs to one ambient function.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev MultiOperator (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (r : ℕ) : Type _

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Specializes MultiOperator to one input slot.
Specializes MultiOperator to two input slots; this is the ambient bidifferential-operator carrier type, without a differential property yet.
Specializes MultiOperator to three input slots.
Turns a unary ambient operator P into a rank-one multi-operator by applying P to the unique input F(0).
correspondence.leanType: def unaryToMulti (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) : CalConf.MultiOperator 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Turns a rank-one multi-operator A into a unary operator by supplying the constant one-slot tuple with value f.
correspondence.leanType: def multiToUnary (P : CalConf.MultiOperator 1) : Function Conf → Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Unfolds unaryToMulti on a rank-one input tuple.
correspondence.leanType: theorem unaryToMulti_apply (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) (F : CalConf.MultiInput 1) : CalConf.unaryToMulti P F = P (F 0)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Unfolds multiToUnary on an ambient function.
correspondence.leanType: theorem multiToUnary_apply (P : CalConf.MultiOperator 1) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.multiToUnary P f = P (fun _ => f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Replaces the ith entry of an r-tuple F by f and leaves all other slots unchanged.
correspondence.leanType: def replace {r : ℕ} (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (i : Fin r) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.MultiInput r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Applies one unary operator P separately to every component of a multi-input.
correspondence.leanType: def mapUnary {r : ℕ} (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : CalConf.MultiInput r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: At the replaced slot, the updated tuple evaluates to the new function f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem replace_same {r : ℕ} (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (i : Fin r) (f : Function Conf) : replace F i f i = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluating the mapped tuple at i gives P applied to the original ith input.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mapUnary_apply {r : ℕ} (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (i : Fin r) : mapUnary P F i = P (F i)

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: This is syntax-level scaffold code. MultiOperator is an arbitrary function between function spaces: the file does not require multilinearity, differential dependence, symmetry, locality, homogeneity, or tangentiality.
